tldr: During the quarter, this cohort of elite investors rotated heavily into semiconductor infrastructure and wide-moat financial services while taking profits in select mega-cap tech and consumer discretionary names. The consensus moves reveal a distinct preference for the 'picks and shovels' of the AI buildout via chipmakers and industry ETFs, alongside high-margin payment and data monopolies. These additions were largely funded by trimming widely held software giants like Microsoft and consumer-facing stocks like Target and Chipotle.

themes.heading: Leaning Into Semiconductors & AI Hardware
themes.detail: Investors aggressively initiated and added to semiconductor manufacturers and broad industry ETFs, signaling a strong conviction in the hardware layer of the tech sector. SMH saw 3 initiations, while individual chipmakers like Broadcom, Micron, and Taiwan Semiconductor were heavily bought.

themes.heading: Accumulating High-Margin Financials & Data
themes.detail: The roster showed a clear appetite for wide-moat payment networks and credit rating agencies. Visa was the most-bought individual stock with 4 managers adding or initiating, complemented by strong buying in data monopolies S&P Global and Moody's.

themes.heading: Trimming Mega-Cap Tech & Consumer Discretionary
themes.detail: Managers took profits in heavily owned mega-cap software and consumer-facing names. Microsoft was the most-sold stock across the board with 5 managers trimming, while consumer discretionary names like Target, Chipotle, and MercadoLibre also saw significant selling pressure.

themes.heading: Niche Media & Industrial Initiations
themes.detail: A surprising consensus emerged around specific media and industrial equipment plays. Versant Media Group tied for the most-bought stock overall with 4 pure initiations, while industrial names DNOW and Sunbelt Rentals each saw 2 managers initiate new positions.
